title: Reporters Pretend to Be Gay for Asylum Help
summary: A BBC investigation found that some immigration advisers are helping migrants cheat the asylum system in the UK. They tell migrants to pretend to be gay to get asylum. A BBC reporter met an adviser named Tanisa Khan, who offered to give fake evidence, like letters and photos, to make it look like the reporter was gay. Migrants then apply for asylum, saying they are scared to go back to countries like Pakistan or Bangladesh. The Home Office says people who cheat will be punished and could be removed from the UK. This story shows how some people try to trick the system to stay in the UK.

things like letters or photos that are not real and are used to trick people

background_read: Asylum is when a country protects people who are in danger in their home country. In the UK, some people apply for asylum if they are scared to go back. This story is about how some advisers are helping migrants cheat by pretending to be gay, which is a group that might face danger in some places. The BBC did an undercover investigation to find out more.
Article_Structure: WHO: BBC reporter and immigration adviser Tanisa Khan
WHAT: Advisers help migrants cheat by pretending to be gay for asylum
WHY: To show how some people try to trick the asylum system

perspectives.description: The Home Office says cheating is wrong and people will be punished.
The BBC found evidence of advisers helping migrants cheat.
